title: Logs, Events, Metrics, Inspect, Debugging, and Incident Evidence · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Logs Drivers Rotation
summary: A service restarts intermittently with no useful application error while disk, memory, network, and health evidence disagree. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker logs drivers rotation increment in layered Docker incident evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy.
Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example container exits only after revision 2; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity. Competency probe: Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy. A defensible solution applies Docker Logs Drivers Rotation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered Docker incident evidence packet.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-logs-drivers-rotation — Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-observability-debugging-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-observability-debugging-3
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked docker observability debugging 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      baseline: ps-inspect-events-logs-stats
      correlation: release-digest-and-container-id
      mutation-policy: reproduce-before-exec
      diagnostic-image: separately-reviewed
      conclusion: first-causal-layer
    verify: docker inspect failed-api --format json && docker logs --timestamps failed-api
    failure: Reject interactive mutation as diagnosis, unbounded log collection, high-cardinality labels, or a restart presented as root-cause evidence.
    repair: Preserve state and timestamps, compare desired with observed configuration, isolate one layer, reproduce cleanly, and retain the causal regression.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
